prose:
Method–provenance block  
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for furan; headline value is f⁺ on the most electrophilic atom (C5).
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF 2.4.0.
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental/cartesian coordinates taken from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz`; no re-optimization at DFT (single-point property evaluation).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet) for the neutral; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states were built automatically for the N ± 1 calculations.
Software/backend: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill invoking PySCF for DFT single-point energies and Mulliken charge analysis.
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for the N, N+1, and N–1 states all reported as converged in the live log; density fitting (RI-JK) used.
Warnings (verbatim from engine, surfaced above):

 • “sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)”
 • “common header present”
These warnings indicate the internal charge-conservation checks passed (sums exactly 1.0000).
